questions.question: What are leading lines in photography?
options: Lines in the scene that guide the viewer's eye through the image
Lines added to the photo in editing only
The edges of the camera sensor
Lines on the lens that show focal length
questions.reviewHint: Leading lines are real elements in the scene that draw the eye through the frame, often toward the subject.

questions.question: Which of these commonly acts as a leading line?
options: A road, path, fence or river
questions.reviewHint: Paths, roads, walls, rivers and similar features are classic leading lines.

questions.question: A main reason to use leading lines is to…
options: Create a sense of depth and draw attention to the subject
questions.reviewHint: Leading lines add depth and direct the viewer toward the point of interest.

questions.question: Diagonal leading lines tend to make a composition feel…
questions.reviewHint: Diagonals add a dynamic, energetic quality compared with flat horizontal lines.

questions.question: Where should leading lines ideally direct the viewer?
options: Toward the main subject or point of interest
Off the edge of the frame away from the subject
questions.reviewHint: Effective leading lines guide the eye to the subject, not away from it.

questions.question: A common mistake with leading lines is…
options: Lines that lead the eye out of the frame or to nothing
questions.reviewHint: Avoid distracting lines that pull attention away from the subject or out of the frame.

questions.question: Converging lines (like a road narrowing into the distance) are useful because they…
options: Create a strong sense of depth and perspective
questions.reviewHint: Converging lines exaggerate depth and pull the eye into the scene.

questions.question: Leading lines don't have to be straight. They can also be…
options: Curves, such as a winding river or path
questions.reviewHint: Curved lines (S-curves, winding paths) work as leading lines too and feel more natural.

questions.question: To position leading lines well, photographers often…
options: Change their viewpoint or angle so the lines run toward the subject
Raise the ISO as high as possible
questions.reviewHint: Moving your position changes how lines run through the frame - use it to aim them at the subject.

questions.question: Leading lines can also support a photograph by…
options: Helping tell a story and connecting elements in the scene
questions.reviewHint: Lines can link foreground to subject and add to the visual narrative.
